revision.text:
[[ja:行燈山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Andon-yama Kofun (行燈山古墳) | image = File:Andon-yama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11534332|1=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.557458|N|135.849094|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length: 242m
Height: 31m (rear circular mound) | built = Early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} | burial = Presumed {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}: Emperor Sujin (10th Emperor) | burial goods = Copper plate, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | Mausoleum = {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi no Magari no Okaue Tomb|ja|山辺道勾岡上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi no Magari no Okaue Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notes = 16th largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Ranking (National Edition)] (Sakai City official site, 2018). | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=27|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=57.48|label=Andon-yama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|27|N|135|50|57.48|E|qq=}}|qq=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} }} '''Andon-yama Kofun''' (行燈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Yanagimoto-chō. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. Although the actual buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designation1 it as the tomb of Emperor Sujin (10th Emperor) under the name '''Yamanobe no Michi no Magari no Okaue Tomb''' (山辺道勾岡上陵). The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} (early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}), Ranking as the 16th largest tumulus in Japan. == Overview == Andon-yama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. During the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, restoration work by the Yanagimoto Domain altered some features such as the surrounding moat. The tumulus is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency as an imperial tomb, with several excavations conducted in the past, including those by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}} in the 1970s and an academic investigation in 2017. The tumulus has a total length of 242 meters, making it the 16th largest tumulus in Japan and the second-largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group, following the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300m). The mound is three-tiered, and features such as stone coverings and haniwa were identified. The burial facility is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular mound. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from Andon-yama Kofun include: * A copper plate measuring 70 cm by 53.8 cm, found during Edo-period restoration works. The plate features designs similar to the [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]] on one side and a grid pattern on the other. * Cylindrical haniwa, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery, identified during excavations by the Imperial Household Agency. The copper plate is particularly notable, although its current location is unknown. A rubbing of the plate remains as a historical record. == Related Sites == The following sites are associated with Andon-yama Kofun: * {{ill|Ando-yama Kofun (Inner Tomb)|ja|アンド山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ando-yama Kofun (Inner Tomb)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626169}}: A nearby tomb considered to be a satellite Kofun of Andon-yama Kofun, measuring 120m in length. * {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Ando-yama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626169}}: Another satellite tomb, with a length of 65m. == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} * {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|行燈山古墳|version=99994836|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Andon-yama Kofun}} [[ceb:Andon-yama Kofun]] [[ja:行燈山古墳]] [[fr:Andon-yama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Andon-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11626169}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:大和青垣国定公園]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:山辺の道]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:柳本古墳群]]
